mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative risk sentiment and downward pressure on equity prices within one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of instances where the unemployment rate rises by over 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, which sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive equity market performance over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns (Sharpe ratio of signal-weighted portfolio < 0.5 over rolling 5-year windows).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase indicates capacity constraints and slowing output growth, which compresses profit margins and forces equity valuations lower over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in over 60% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) outside late-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the consumer price index below 0.3% year-over-year signals disinflationary pressure that removes central bank tightening constraints, prompting immediate positive equity repricing within one week.
failure_condition: Equity markets exhibit negative or flat 5-day returns following CPIAUCSL YoY drops below 0.3% in more than 50% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment rates indicates structural labor market deterioration rather than cyclical fluctuation, which erodes long-term earnings expectations and produces negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a positive risk-adjusted return over 60-day horizons, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real gross domestic product reflect expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, which directly translates into higher equity valuations and positive market returns over the following month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns across multiple rolling 3-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals rapidly improving supply chain efficiency and capacity utilization, which triggers short-term bullish momentum and positive equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration during post-shock recovery periods fails to generate positive 5-day forward returns, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance (Hit Rate < 50%).
